refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity output of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: operator capability (on-site vs. remote), task execution, measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Require independent audit of telemetry data and compliance checklist sign-off by Safety Manager.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall transmit operator commands and receive vehicle state telemetry with latency ≤ acceptable_threshold to enable safe remote task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall monitor task execution and operator actions, detect unsafe conditions, and enforce worksite safety plan constraints in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall aggregate productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, cycle time, utilization) and report against productivity_target for evaluation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Equip Vehicle with high-capacity bucket, optimized hydraulics, and cycle-time-minimized control logic; enable on-site operator control via standard cab interface; measure output via load-cell and GPS.

DP-B: Equip Vehicle with standard bucket and hydraulics; enable dual-mode operation (on-site cab + remote teleoperation); integrate Teleoperation system for command/telemetry relay; integrate Safety Services for real-time constraint enforcement; aggregate metrics via NGHE Solution dashboard.

DP-C: Equip Vehicle with minimal on-board autonomy; rely entirely on Teleoperation system for all task commands; integrate Safety Services with veto authority; implement high-bandwidth Network Link for low-latency telemetry; measure productivity via integrated sensors and cloud-based analytics.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Dual-mode operation (on-site + remote teleoperation) with integrated Safety Services constraint enforcement and NGHE Solution-level productivity aggregation.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle-time performance is independent of teleoperation latency (on-site mode decouples this).
Safety Services constraint enforcement is orthogonal to productivity measurement (safety gates task execution, does not directly affect cycle time).
Teleoperation and Safety Services both depend on Network Link, but this is a shared infrastructure dependency, not a functional coupling.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits low coupling: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services can be designed and tested independently, with integration points at the NGHE Solution level (tasking, telemetry aggregation, safety alert routing). This supports modular development and risk mitigation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: On-site operator can achieve productivity_target without teleoperation; teleoperation is an alternative mode, not a requirement for baseline productivity.
Assumption: Safety Services can enforce constraints without introducing unacceptable latency or task interruption.
Risk: If Network Link bandwidth or latency is insufficient, remote teleoperation mode may not be viable; on-site mode becomes primary, reducing flexibility.
Risk: Safety Services veto authority may conflict with productivity targets if constraints are overly conservative; requires stakeholder alignment on acceptable safety/productivity trade-off.
Risk: Productivity measurement accuracy depends on sensor calibration and data integrity; poor telemetry quality will invalidate KPI evaluation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify with Site Manager and Safety Manager: (1) Is on-site operation mandatory as a fallback? (2) What is the acceptable latency threshold for remote teleoperation? (3) Are there specific safety constraints that may limit cycle time? (4) What is the required accuracy and audit trail for productivity measurement?

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems; aggregating productivity metrics; enforcing operational policies; and providing reporting interface to Site Manager and Project Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives vehicle state (position, payload, cycle status), operator actions (command history), and safety events from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Used for productivity calculation and audit trail.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Transmits task assignments (dig location, load target, dump zone) to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Enforces task sequencing and worksite layout constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time safety alerts and constraint violations from Safety Services. Triggers task suspension, operator notification, or emergency stop as required.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides communication backbone for all inter-subsystem messaging (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert). Supports both on-site (local network) and remote (WAN) operation modes.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for this FR. It owns the productivity KPI, orchestrates subsystems, and provides the reporting interface. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link infrastructure is shared with subsystems.

Vehicle owns cycle-time performance and payload capacity, but does not own teleoperation, safety constraint enforcement, or productivity aggregation. Assigning FR to Vehicle alone would require Vehicle to implement teleoperation and safety logic, causing high diffusion and tight coupling.

Teleoperation owns remote command/telemetry relay, but does not own on-site operation, safety enforcement, or productivity measurement. Assigning FR to Teleoperation alone would exclude on-site mode and require Teleoperation to implement safety and measurement logic.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement, but does not own vehicle control, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning FR to Safety Services would be a category error: safety is a gate, not a productivity driver.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because: (1) it owns the productivity KPI and its measurement; (2) it orchestrates the dual-mode operation (on-site + remote); (3) it integrates Safety Services constraints into task execution; (4) it provides the reporting interface to stakeholders. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are subsystems that contribute to the FR but do not own it.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is too thin (only a data aggregator), it may not enforce task sequencing or safety constraints effectively. Mitigation: Define NGHE Solution as an active orchestrator, not a passive logger.

Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services are not properly integrated into NGHE Solution, productivity measurement may be incomplete or inaccurate. Mitigation: Define clear interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) and implement data validation at NGHE Solution level.

Risk: If Network Link fails, NGHE Solution cannot aggregate telemetry or enforce safety alerts. Mitigation: Define fallback modes (e.g., on-site operation with local safety monitoring) and require Network Link redundancy for remote operation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm with System Engineer and Project Manager: (1) Is NGHE Solution responsible for task scheduling and worksite layout management, or is this a Site Manager responsibility? (2) What is the required data retention and audit trail for productivity reporting? (3) Are there regulatory or contractual requirements for independent verification of productivity metrics?

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (dual-mode operation with integrated Safety Services and NGHE Solution aggregation) directly addresses the refined FR. It enables on-site and remote task execution, enforces safety constraints, and provides productivity measurement. The DP is complete, feasible, and testable.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface must be implemented by Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; NGHE Solution must aggregate and validate. Risk: inconsistent telemetry formats or missing data fields. Mitigation: Define telemetry schema and validation rules at NGHE Solution level.

Tasking interface must be implemented by Vehicle and Teleoperation; NGHE Solution must enforce constraints. Risk: task conflicts or unsafe task sequences. Mitigation: Implement task validation and sequencing logic in NGHE Solution.
SafetyAlert interface must be implemented by Safety Services; NGHE Solution must respond. Risk: delayed or missed alerts. Mitigation: Implement alert routing and escalation logic in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container for DP-B. It owns the orchestration logic, productivity measurement, and safety constraint enforcement. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are subsystems that implement specific DPs (vehicle control, command relay, constraint monitoring) and expose interfaces to NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trial. Productivity metrics can be measured from Telemetry interface (volume, tonnage, cycle time). Safety compliance can be verified from SafetyAlert interface (no constraint violations during evaluation period). Operator mode (on-site vs. remote) can be logged and correlated with productivity.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the FR and its verification. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1, FR-NGHE-001.2, FR-NGHE-001.3) and their verification. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined and testable.

recommended_revisions: Define acceptance criteria for productivity_target: Is it a single value (e.g., 50 m³/h) or a range? Is it mode-dependent (on-site vs. remote)? Is it weather or soil-dependent?
Define evaluation period: Is it a single 8-hour shift, a 5-day week, or a longer period? Are there seasonal variations?
Define safety compliance criteria: Are there specific incidents (near-miss, injury, equipment damage) that would invalidate the evaluation?
Define telemetry accuracy requirements: What is the acceptable error margin for volume and tonnage measurement? What is the required audit trail for regulatory compliance?

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic; it has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) that address distinct concerns: vehicle performance, teleoperation capability, safety constraint enforcement, and productivity aggregation. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define DPs and IMs. The current level (NGHE Solution) is the correct IM boundary for the top-level FR, but the sub-FRs must be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively, and each must be decomposed further.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall transmit operator commands and receive vehicle state telemetry with latency ≤ acceptable_threshold to enable safe remote task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall monitor task execution and operator actions, detect unsafe conditions, and enforce worksite safety plan constraints in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall aggregate productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, cycle time, utilization) and report against productivity_target for evaluation.

assumptions: On-site operator can achieve productivity_target without teleoperation; teleoperation is an alternative mode.
Safety Services can enforce constraints without introducing unacceptable latency or task interruption.
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for remote teleoperation operation.
Productivity measurement is based on aggregated telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems.
Site Manager and Safety Manager will provide acceptance criteria for productivity_target and safety compliance during requirements refinement.
open_risks: Network Link bandwidth or latency may be insufficient for remote teleoperation; on-site mode becomes primary.
Safety Services constraint enforcement may conflict with productivity targets; requires stakeholder alignment on acceptable trade-off.
Productivity measurement accuracy depends on sensor calibration and data integrity; poor telemetry quality will invalidate KPI evaluation.
Evaluation period and acceptance criteria for productivity_target are not yet defined; may require multiple trials or seasonal adjustments.
Regulatory or contractual requirements for independent verification of productivity metrics are not yet identified.
evidence_needed: Acceptance criteria for productivity_target (value, range, mode-dependency, weather/soil-dependency).
Evaluation period definition (duration, frequency, seasonal variations).
Safety compliance criteria (incident types, thresholds, audit trail requirements).
Telemetry accuracy requirements (error margins, sensor specifications, data validation rules).
Network Link specifications (bandwidth, latency, redundancy, fallback modes).
Regulatory and contractual requirements for productivity reporting and audit trail.
Stakeholder alignment on safety/productivity trade-offs and constraint enforcement policies.
